column1: # text = Who is going to build Braunschweig's low-cost housing?
# text = The SPD and the Greens think that investors should step up to the plate much more in the future.
# text = How to react to the bad news that Braunschweig will lose almost 1300 low-cost homes over the next few years?
# text = At the next council meeting, the SPD is going to put forward an "action plan for homes".
# text = But it won't be smooth sailing.
# text = Firstly, there is the question of who is actually going to build the low-cost homes?
# text = So far, no settlement has been reached on this point.

# text = SPD group chairman, Christoph Bratmann, says: "We agree with the Greens: if you want to build on municipal land, you will have to designate 20 percent of all homes as social housing in the future."
# text = The low-cost homes could also be older homes that are renovated.
# text = However, the main issue for the council, according to Bratmann:
# text = "How do we handle investors who want to build on private ground?"
# text = We want investors building on those spaces to keep at least 10 percent, but if possible, 20 percent, of homes as social housing.\"
# text = A new municipal programme to promote the construction of homes, which will cost millions, and which still needs to be financed, will trigger concerns.
# text = The problem, according to Bratmann: \"At the moment, there is no agreement between groups.\"
# text = The CDU wants to put as few rules on investors as possible.
# text = The Left wants more stringent requirements to be imposed on investors.
# text = Despite that, according to Bratman, the situation in terms of the housing market is also hotting up.
# text = If the action plan is implemented, the building authorities will be faced with additional tasks.
# text = More staff will be needed.
# text = It is not clear if we will get the specialist staff.
# text = But the program is not quite complete: because the approximately 1260 new homes are to be built anyway.
# text = But how do you balance out the loss of an additional 1300 low-cost homes (approximately), the grants for which will run out over the next few years?
# text = This will come to 500 homes even by 2020.
# text = To prevent this, according to Nicole Palm, spokeswoman for planning policy, bonds that are about to mature should be purchased by the municipality.
# text = In addition, thought must be given to putting homes back into a price maintenance scenario.
# text = This is possible in the west of the city.
# text = The problem could be solved in one fell swoop.
# text = This is because deadlines expire at the end of the year in the west of the city.
# text = Around 1800 homes could then be moved into the managed price scheme for affordable housing.
# text = The only problem is that their owners, housing companies, are against it.
# text = This would dramatically increase the amount of affordable housing in the west of the city, taking a step back from the target of \"blending\".
# text = No longer are socially disadvantaged people to be concentrated in a small number of districts.
# text = The Greens think there's still room for manoeuvre in the discussions.
# text = Group chairman Holger Herlitschke thinks that "the CDU will have to get into bed with us to reach a decent majority."
# text = However, they have focused on providing more building sites for detached homes.
# text = But that does not mean that it will be impossible to reach a compromise.
